name: Mechanism forcing N_families = 3 from structural inputs
closability_note: Multiple compactifications produce three families (heterotic Euler characteristic, F-theory intersection numbers, flipped SU(5)) but none is unique — each yields 3 from a discrete moduli choice that doesn't rule out 2, 4, or other counts.
summary: No framework derives N_families = 3 from structural inputs. The SM takes it as a postulate; GUTs unify within a family; string compactifications produce three-family models without forcing N=3.
what_closes_it: A mechanism that forces N=3 with no remaining degree of freedom: a topological constraint ruling out other values, a flavor symmetry whose representations demand three families (modular Gamma_3 at specific weight is the active route), or an environmental selection over a finite measure.
scale_hint: GUT scale or higher (10^16 - 10^18 GeV). The generation count is set in compactification or by symmetry at the highest scales; low-energy mechanisms cannot change the number of chiral fermion families.

principles_at_stake.note: Anomaly cancellation in the SM works at any generation count; the gap-closer would have to add a constraint beyond gauge anomaly (e.g. a stringy tadpole condition, a modular-symmetry constraint, or a global symmetry's anomaly).

literature_anchors.citation: Candelas, Horowitz, Strominger, Witten, 'Vacuum configurations for superstrings', Nucl. Phys. B 258, 46 (1985)
Donagi and Wijnholt, 'Model Building with F-Theory', Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 15, 1237 (2011)
Marsano, Saulina, Schafer-Nameki, 'Compact F-theory GUTs with U(1)_PQ', JHEP 04 (2010) 095
Ding and King, 'Neutrino mass and mixing with modular symmetry', Rept. Prog. Phys. 87, 084201 (2024)
